response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior generalist with strong web, TypeScript/Node.js, and DLang systems experience, but he does not meet the role's core mandatory requirements. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. His database background is MySQL/MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and he has no fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain exposure — RIDE Capital was a German investment-administration fintech, not a capital-markets environment. The low-latency work at Dunnhumby shows relevant performance-engineering instincts, but the stack (DLang, Linux) does not transfer directly to this Windows C++ fixed-income role.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, desktop, mobile, and embedded systems
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of requests/second) using asynchronous I/O and caching
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and sustained open-source contributions
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience (2014-2015), versus the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; SQL background is limited to MySQL/MongoDB
No fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain knowledge, which the role requires

key_factors.factor: Missing capital markets / fixed income domain
Strong general engineering and low-latency background
